is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum infections; 903 microscopy-confirmed P. falciparum infections sequenced.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfk13 (R622I, C580Y), Pfcrt (72,74,75,76,271,326,371 etc.), Pfdhfr (51,59,108), Pfdhps (436,437,540,581,613), Pfmdr1 (86). Supplementary tables give per-locus counts.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: S1/S2/S3 tables give n mutant / N sequenced and % per marker overall, per district, and per Kebele (e.g. R622I 369/833 44.3%; crt K76T 586/799 73.37%; dhfr N51I 806/811).

sub_country_location.evidence: Table S3 gives prevalence per Kebele (26 named Kebeles) with n/N; S1 Data includes GPS coordinates per Kebele. Data resolvable well below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Study Nov 2022–Oct 2023 (one year). S4-S6 tables provide monthly R622I prevalence with n/N; overall window <=3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates from Gondar Zuria and Tach Armachiho districts, Amhara region, northwest Ethiopia; health-facility cross-sectional study.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary genomic surveillance study with MIP sequencing of field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: New field collection Nov 2022–Oct 2023; part of East Africa Consortium for Clinical Research (EACCR) funding and same region/method as prior Fola 2023 and Brhane 2025 studies, but these are new samples from a defined new period. Some methodological continuity but distinct sample set.

supplement.evidence: Supplementary tables S1-S6 and S1 Data with per-locus counts, per-Kebele breakdowns, monthly prevalence, and GPS coordinates are all provided as text.

assessment.summary: Genomic surveillance of 903 P. falciparum infections from Gondar Zuria and Tach Armachiho districts (Amhara, NW Ethiopia), Nov 2022–Oct 2023, using MIP sequencing. Reports extractable per-locus frequencies (n/N) for k13, crt, dhfr, dhps, mdr1 markers overall, per district, per Kebele, and per month, with GPS coordinates. Meets all eligibility criteria.
